name: River Cess County (Liberia)
description: White field bordered top and bottom by blue stripes; the Liberian national flag forms the canton in the upper hoist (over the upper blue stripe), and the fly half shows a green-leaved tree with a brown trunk amid yellow sun-rays, set against the white field.
history: Presented by President William V. S. Tubman on 29 November 1965 together with the flags of the other Liberian counties.

features.symbolism: River Cess as part of the Republic of Liberia
a tree, with rays of the rising sun, representing the natural endowment and prospect of the county
